title: Classic Cubby Series — 9 Cubby Bookshelf (Face-Framed)
summary: A double-face-framed cubby bookcase — a 1x12 box with matching front AND back 1x2 face frames, and shelves on pocket holes or shelf pins instead of a plywood back.
description:
Despite the same nine-opening layout as this catalog's other cubby bookshelf, this is a mechanically different build: there's no plywood back at all, and instead the box gets face frames on BOTH the front and the back, built as their own flat assemblies before either one ever touches the carcass. Building both frames flat, on the bench, keeps their joints tight in a way that fitting frame pieces directly onto an assembled box does not.

The back face frame goes on first, attached to the box with finish nails and glue, and only then does the front face frame get built the same way and attached over the front opening — building them in that order, back before front, means the second frame can be checked and adjusted against a box that's already square rather than compounding two sets of possible misalignment at once.

Shelves have a genuine choice here: pocket holes on the underside for a fixed, permanent shelf, or shelf pins drilled with a shelf-pin jig for adjustable shelving. Optional moulding, cut to fit rather than to a fixed length, dresses up the face frames further if wanted — this piece leans more toward furniture-grade presentation than the simpler plywood-backed version, which is the real distinction between the two.

materials.name: 1x2, 8 ft
materials.note: Both face frames — front and back.

materials.name: Optional moulding
materials.note: Cut to fit the assembled face frames.

steps.title: Build the box
steps.body: Build the box from the sides, top, bottom, and middle uprights with 3/4" pocket holes, 1-1/4" pocket hole screws, and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the back face frame
steps.body: Build the back face frame flat on the bench with a pocket-hole jig, then attach it to the back of the box with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Build and attach the front face frame
steps.body: Build the front face frame the same way as the back, then attach with pocket holes to the box front, secured further with 1-1/4" finish nails and glue.

steps.title: Add optional moulding
steps.body: Add moulding to the face frames if wanted, cut to fit the assembled frames rather than to a fixed length.

steps.title: Install the shelves and finish
steps.body: Install the shelves either with pocket holes on the underside — two per end, near the outside edges, with 1-1/4" pocket hole screws — for fixed shelving, or with shelf pins drilled using a shelf-pin jig for adjustable shelving. Sand and finish.

images.alt: Face-framed 9-cubby bookshelf with front and back trim frames
